title: World Syncer
description: World Syncer is a mod that keeps your game world data synchronized across devices for smoother cross-device experience or as a backup system.
body:
# **World Syncer**

World Syncer is a Minecraft mod designed to seamlessly synchronize your world data across multiple devices. Whether you're switching between computers or running multiple sessions, World Syncer ensures your world is always up-to-date and accessible wherever and whenever you play.

## **Features**

- **Automatic Synchronization:** Keeps your Minecraft world data in sync across devices with minimal setup.
- **Cross-Platform Support:** Compatible with all major platforms that support Minecraft Java Edition.
- **Lightweight & Efficient:** Minimal performance impact, focusing only on world and essential data—no unnecessary overhead.

## **Usage**

1. **Install the Mod:** Download and add World Syncer to your mods folder.
2. **Get your Github access token via [GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens (classic)](https://github.com/settings/tokens) - more help in the projects github repo
3. **Leave and Relaunch Minecraft
4. **Multi-Device Play:** Install World Syncer on all devices you use. Log in with the same access token to keep worlds aligned.

## **Dependencies**

- **Minecraft Java Edition:** Version 1.20.1 or any 1.21.x
- **Fabric API
- **Cloth Config API
- **Mod Menu (optional)
- **Java 17 or higher**
- **Github access token (via Usage)

## **Compatibility**

- If some parts of the config screen are blured turn off menu blur in video settings
- Works with most other mods.
- Designed for singleplayer enviroment.
- No known conflicts, but always back up your world before first use.

## **Additional Information**

- **Open Source:** Contributions are welcome! Check out the GitHub repo for details.
- **Support:** For help or to report bugs, use the issues tab on GitHub.
- **Regular Updates:** Stay tuned for new features like scheduled syncs, more cloud providers, and advanced conflict management.
